entities.context: V's company, referenced in system context

entities.name: Workout Tracker DB
entities.context: SQLite database at Personal/Health/workouts.db for logging and querying workout data

entities.name: SQLite
entities.context: Database engine used for local workout storage

entities.name: Fitbit
entities.context: Referenced as a source type in workout_observations table for importing fitness data

entities.name: N5
entities.context: V's workspace orchestration and knowledge system, contextual to this script's environment

entities.name: workouts table
entities.context: Canonical table storing date, modality, duration, distance, calories, heart rate metrics, and notes

entities.name: workout_observations table
entities.context: Source-level observation records linked to workouts; supports Fitbit, manual text, photo, and other input sources

entities.name: primary_modality
entities.context: Field capturing the type of workout (e.g., running, cycling) in the canonical workouts table

entities.name: Heart Rate Tracking
entities.context: avg_hr and peak_hr columns track cardiovascular intensity per workout

entities.name: Schema Migration
entities.context: Script includes forward-compatible migration logic (e.g., adding peak_hr column if missing)

entities.name: Personal Health System
entities.context: Broader health tracking domain under Personal/Health/ that this DB serves

relationships.context: V tracks personal fitness data using this SQLite-based workout database
The workout tracker is built on SQLite for local, file-based storage
Fitbit is a named data source in the workout_observations table for importing external fitness data
Observations link to workouts via workout_id foreign key; multiple sources can feed one canonical workout
avg_hr and peak_hr fields in the workouts table capture cardiovascular metrics
The init_db function includes PRAGMA-based migration to add peak_hr column to existing databases
The DB lives under Personal/Health/ as part of V's broader health tracking infrastructure
V maintains a personal health system including workout tracking, nutrition, and bio-logging
